title: GPT Image 2 Prompt Templates From Viral X Examples | Toolaze
description: Copy source-backed GPT Image 2 prompt templates from X for ads, posters, memes, UI mockups, portraits, infographics, and social images.
heroTitle: Create GPT Image 2 Images From Prompt Structures That Already Worked On X
heroDescription: GPT Image 2 prompts are often strongest when they describe layout, subject, style, and constraints in one clean brief. This page collects real X examples so you can copy the structure and make your own image faster.
howToTitle: How to use GPT Image 2 prompt templates
howToIntro: Image prompts need sharper composition rules than video prompts. Start with the visual hierarchy, then add style and constraints.

steps.title: Pick the output type
steps.text: Choose a template for a poster, ad, meme, portrait, infographic, UI board, or product image before changing the subject.

steps.title: Keep the composition logic
steps.text: Preserve the layout instruction that made the source useful, such as hero subject, margins, framing, or text placement.

steps.title: Replace the reference details
steps.text: Swap in your product, character, scene, brand mood, or visual joke while keeping the prompt readable.

steps.title: Check text and artifacts
steps.text: If the image contains typography, keep it short and ask for clean spelling, contrast, and layout.

remixTitle: Use the prompt as an image brief, not a keyword pile
remixText: A strong GPT Image 2 prompt reads like a creative brief. It says what should be seen first, what style should hold the image together, and what must not break.

patterns.label: For ads
patterns.heading: Give the product a job in the image.
patterns.body: Do not only ask for a beautiful packshot. Show the product benefit, setting, material, or moment of use.

patterns.label: For memes
patterns.heading: Preserve the joke structure.
patterns.body: The viral part is often the mismatch, redraw style, or absurd instruction. Keep that mechanic when changing the subject.

patterns.label: For layouts
patterns.heading: Control hierarchy before decoration.
patterns.body: Posters, UI boards, and infographics need spacing and alignment rules before style words.

chooserTitle: Choose a GPT Image 2 template by image format
chooserIntro: Different image formats need different prompt structure.

chooserRows.startWith: A clear hero product and one benefit.
chooserRows.bestFor: Ecommerce, launch posts, skincare, food, fashion, and app promos.
chooserRows.replace: Product, surface, background, use case, and brand mood.
chooserRows.keep: Packshot clarity, material details, and readable final composition.

chooserRows.startWith: A strong title area and one central subject.
chooserRows.bestFor: Campaign images, character posters, event graphics, and social covers.
chooserRows.replace: Subject, title, palette, scene, and typography style.
chooserRows.keep: Grid, margins, contrast, and visual hierarchy.

chooserRows.startWith: The funny instruction or image transformation.
chooserRows.bestFor: Shareable X posts, reaction images, visual jokes, and trend formats.
chooserRows.replace: Subject, reference, joke, and output style.

answerTitle: What is the fastest way to use GPT Image 2 prompts?
answerText: Choose a source-backed template close to your image format, keep its composition rules, replace the subject and style details, then generate a version with a clear first-read focal point. For posters or ads, check text, margins, and product clarity before posting.
anatomyTitle: What a reusable GPT Image 2 prompt usually contains
anatomyIntro: The best reusable image prompts behave like compact art direction.

anatomy.title: Main subject
anatomy.text: Name the object, character, scene, or idea that should be noticed first.

anatomy.title: Composition
anatomy.text: Describe framing, hierarchy, margins, background, and text placement when needed.

anatomy.title: Visual language
anatomy.text: Keep style, lighting, material, color, and perspective consistent.

anatomy.title: Quality controls
anatomy.text: Add constraints for text, anatomy, logos, hands, and unwanted artifacts only when relevant.

trustTitle: Why these GPT Image 2 templates are source-backed
trustText: Prompt libraries get weak when they invent polished-sounding templates. These examples stay useful because the source context is visible.
trustRules: Every template should point back to a public X source, not a made-up prompt example.
The visible prompt should match the original post or comment language instead of being rewritten.
The template should include enough context for a beginner to copy, replace, and test it.
The media and metrics are treated as signals, but the prompt itself must still be usable.
mistakesText: Most failures come from vague layout, too much style stacking, or ignored text constraints.

mistakes.title: Changing the product but not the scene
mistakes.text: A perfume layout may not fit a sneaker, SaaS screen, or food product without changing the setting.

mistakes.title: Asking for too much text
mistakes.text: Short, high-contrast copy works better than paragraphs inside the image.

mistakes.title: Treating style as the whole prompt
mistakes.text: Style words help, but composition and subject hierarchy do more work.

faqs.q: Can I copy these prompts directly?
faqs.a: Yes, but the better workflow is to keep the structure and replace the subject, product, reference, or layout goal.

faqs.q: Do these templates include meme prompts?
faqs.a: Yes. GPT Image 2 examples include ads, posters, portraits, UI concepts, infographics, and meme-style image prompts.

faqs.q: Should I translate the original prompt?
faqs.a: For source accuracy, keep the original prompt language when studying it. You can write your remix in the language your model handles best.
